title: Syracuse Public Safety building could become apartments next to jail and courthouse
summary: Syracuse's Public Safety Building on State Street will be repurposed following the relocation of police and fire departments to a leased facility at 1153 W. Fayette Street by January 2026. Onondaga County is leading an RFP process to redevelop the two-acre downtown property, with housing (apartments) as the preferred use case despite proximity to the county jail and courthouse. The city will pay $1.8M annually in rent for the new headquarters, comparable to current costs.
department_refs: City of Syracuse Department of Assessment

political_signal.description: County leadership actively moving forward on property disposition with RFP process; city characterizing move as cost-effective and efficient; messaging around downtown revitalization suggests economic development priority.

processing_notes: Dollar amount incomplete in mechanical extraction: actual figure is $1.8 million (annual rent)
Deputy Chief Richard Shoff title extracted from context rather than explicit role statement
Article focuses on real estate/facilities strategy; limited direct CIO implications unless digital infrastructure migration to new facility is planned
RFP process underway but no technology procurement angle evident in article text
